auth_notes: Requires Bloomberg Enterprise Web Services (BEWS) for REST access. Authentication typically involves Mutual TLS (mTLS) requiring client certificates and private keys, combined with IP whitelisting.
rate_limits: Strictly governed by the specific Data License agreement; usually limited by 'hits' or 'data points' per month/day.
gotchas: Standard n8n HTTP node requires manual certificate management for mTLS which is difficult to configure in some environments
Bloomberg data is strictly licensed and often tied to specific hardware or IP addresses
The API is not the same as the Terminal software; it requires a separate Enterprise Data subscription
client_must_provide: Whitelisted Static IP of the n8n instance
research_notes: To integrate with n8n, you must use the Bloomberg Enterprise Web Services (REST API). The standard BLPAPI (Desktop API) is not compatible with cloud-based n8n as it requires a local Bloomberg process. You will likely need a custom proxy or a specialized Node.js service to handle the mTLS handshake if the n8n environment's HTTP node is restricted.

effort_recommendation.rationale: To integrate with n8n, you must use the Bloomberg Enterprise Web Services (REST API). The standard BLPAPI (Desktop API) is not compatible with cloud-based n8n as it requires a local Bloomberg process. You will likely need a custom proxy or a specialized Node.js service to handle the mTLS handshake if the n8n environment's HTTP node is restricted.
